findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "tv_explain_recommendation" appears informational but accepts parameters (context) that enable code/file/secret access.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "tv_concierge" is 1598 chars, far longer than this server's median (309). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: You have a window of free time — decide for me what to watch right now. Returns ONE primary decision (TV program OR streaming title) with confidence percentage,…

name: tv_find_for_couple
description: Finds TV programs that satisfy two people with different moods/preferences. Default fairness=strict (min(scoreA,scoreB) >= threshold — no veto). Auto-falls-back to fairness=average if strict returns empty, marked with degraded:true. Returns per-person score breakdown + compromise note when one side

name: tv_explain_recommendation
description: Returns a full score breakdown for a specific program in a given mood/context: per-component value + reason, extracted genres, streaming cross-ref, freshness, sources used, and a list of alternatives that were not picked (with the specific reason each was dropped). Use to understand or debug a recom

name: tv_check_freshness
description: Reports the last-updated time of each underlying data source (EPG normalized, EPG homepage, streaming catalog), the age in minutes, the expected next refresh, and whether any source is stale (older than 1.5× the expected refresh interval).

name: tv_concierge
description: You have a window of free time — decide for me what to watch right now. Returns ONE primary decision (TV program OR streaming title) with confidence percentage, full reasoning breakdown, and up to 3 diverse alternatives with explicit trade-offs (pros/cons, reason not picked). Picks across live Roman

name: tv_important_today
description: What actually matters on Romanian TV today: World Cup / Euro / Champions League matches, finals, knockout games, national-team fixtures. Detected from real EPG text (titles + descriptions) with quoted evidence — the EPG has no structured event metadata, so detection is keyword-based and honest about

name: tv_tonight_card
description: The daily "what is worth watching tonight?" card: one main decision (importance-scored major event, or a deterministic prime-time film fallback) plus one pick per vertical — TV, streaming (official Netflix RO top 10), theater (online + stage union), cinema (box office ∩ today's real screenings) — an

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "tv_explain_recommendation" appears informational but accepts parameters (context) that enable code/file/secret access.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "tv_concierge" is 1598 chars, far longer than this server's median (309). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: You have a window of free time — decide for me what to watch right now. Returns ONE primary decision (TV program OR streaming title) with confidence percentage,…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "tv_concierge" stacks urgency/override words (important, always). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.
